symptoms.symptom_name: Deep painful nodulocystic facial lesions that ruptured with purulent discharge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep tender cysts with rupture are characteristic of nodulocystic acne.
Local purulence/erythema can occur in both; not discriminating alone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Background comedones and seborrhoea on face and upper trunk

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Comedones are the defining lesion of acne and argue strongly against cellulitis, folliculitis and rosacea.
True comedones are not a feature of rosacea.

symptoms.symptom_name: No high fever or rigors (only low-grade 37.6°C)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic febrile response reduces probability of spreading bacterial cellulitis/sepsis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No periorbital spread or visual change

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No advancing periorbital erythema or visual disturbance makes sight-threatening orbital cellulitis very unlikely.
A sharply demarcated, advancing, tender plaque would support cellulitis; its absence on exam supports acne. Requires clinician inspection/palpation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Comedonal background plus absence of systemic febrile response and periorbital spread differentiates a severe acne flare from facial cellulitis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Clinical diagnosis — no routine bloods required
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Acne is a clinical diagnosis. Bloods/imaging are not indicated for a localised flare without systemic features.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bacterial swab of purulent discharge (if not responding)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Consider swab MC&S if poor response to oral antibiotics or to guide therapy where secondary infection is suspected.
Not indicated in the absence of systemic toxicity; ordering them here reflects over-diagnosis of sepsis/cellulitis.
